rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as part of the core theoretical positioning, not just a background list citation. The citing article contrasts its message-driven account with prior people-driven/self-presentation work that includes Berman et al. (2015), explicitly treating bragging and self-promotion as an alternative mechanism to test. It also adapts a self-promotion measure from Berman et al. (2015) in Study 3 to empirically rule out that alternative path. This gives the citation a clear conceptual and measurement role in framing and adjudicating mechanisms.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the implications of Berman et al. (2015) for charitable WOM by showing that, in solicited post-donation sharing, message altruism and perceived persuasion efficacy better explain sharing than self-promotion motives. It operationalizes Berman et al.'s self-promotion construct to test and bound when a bragging/self-presentational account applies.
